watched.title: Arrival
watched.overview: A linguist works to understand visitors whose arrival may reshape humanity's future.
watched.review: A rare science-fiction film where attention and empathy matter more than spectacle. Its structure rewards patience, and the final movement gives every earlier scene a different emotional weight.

watched.title: Moonlight
watched.overview: A young man searches for identity and connection across three chapters of his life.
watched.review: Quiet, precise, and deeply humane. The performances feel connected across time, while the color and music express what the characters cannot say aloud.

watched.title: The Grand Budapest Hotel
watched.overview: A concierge and his lobby-boy protégé become entangled in a vanished painting and a family fortune.
watched.review: The meticulous comedy is irresistible, but the fading world underneath it is what stays. Every visual joke also seems to preserve something already disappearing.

watchlist.title: Perfect Days
watchlist.overview: A Tokyo cleaner finds meaning through routine, music, books, and brief encounters.
watchlist.notes: Seems ideal for a quiet Sunday evening.

watchlist.title: Anatomy of a Fall
watchlist.overview: A writer faces trial after her husband's suspicious death exposes the uncertainty inside their marriage.
watchlist.notes: Recommended for its ambiguity and performances.

watchlist.title: Aftersun
watchlist.overview: Years later, a woman revisits memories of a holiday with her father.
watchlist.notes: Save for when I want something emotionally demanding.

deterministicDiscoveries.title: Columbus
deterministicDiscoveries.overview: Two people form a friendship through architecture, responsibility, and suspended plans.
deterministicDiscoveries.rationale: Your highest ratings favor intimate dramas with precise visual language and patient observation.

deterministicDiscoveries.title: The Worst Person in the World
deterministicDiscoveries.overview: A young woman navigates love, work, and the uncertainty of becoming herself.
deterministicDiscoveries.rationale: It matches your preference for character studies that balance melancholy with sharply observed humor.

aiDiscoveries.title: Petite Maman
aiDiscoveries.overview: A child exploring her mother's childhood home meets a girl building a treehouse nearby.
aiDiscoveries.rationale: Its compact story uses a quiet speculative idea to explore memory and family, echoing what you valued in Arrival and Moonlight.

aiDiscoveries.title: The Lives of Others
aiDiscoveries.overview: A surveillance officer becomes absorbed in the lives of the artists he monitors.
aiDiscoveries.rationale: The moral transformation is gradual and performance-led, with the emotional precision your reviews consistently reward.
